notes: Blue whale blubber k=0.22 W/m-K based on Kvadsheim et al. (1996) measurement of 0.25 W/m-K for minke whale (also rorqual family). Earlier estimates of 0.19-0.20 underestimated heat loss. Blue whales have 5-30 cm blubber, thinner than right/bowhead whales due to streamlined rorqual body plan.
source_reference: Kvadsheim PH, Folkow LP, Blix AS (1996). Thermal conductivity of minke whale blubber. J Therm Biol 21:123-128. DOI: 10.1016/0306-4565(95)00034-8; Blawas AM et al. (2025). Life in the slowest lane: Feeding allometry lowers metabolic rate scaling in the largest whales. Science Advances. DOI: 10.1126/sciadv.adw2232; Lavigne DM, Innes S, Worthy GAJ (1990). Lower critical temperatures of blue whales. J Theor Biol 144:249-257.; Parry DA (1949). The structure of whale blubber (blue and fin whales). Q J Microsc Sci 90:13-25.; Kanwisher J, Sundnes G (1966). Thermal regulation in Cetacea. In: Whales, dolphins, and porpoises. UC Press, pp 397-407.; See docs/blue_whale_references.md for full citation list.

notes: Bowhead blubber conductivity reported ~0.23 W/m-K (North Slope Borough). Resting subcutaneous adipose perfusion ~1.0-1.3 ml/100g/min (human studies; see docs/bowhead_references.md) converted to ~0.18-0.23 kg/(m3·s); use 0.23 as proxy for blubber baseline. Species perfusion_scale models countercurrent heat exchange.
source_reference: North Slope Borough. Bowhead whale energetics and temperature regulation. Blubber conductivity 0.23 W/m-K; deep body temperature ~33.8 C.; Parry DA (1949). The structure of whale blubber. Q J Microsc Sci 90:13-25.; Muller et al. (2014). Circulation. Resting subcutaneous adipose blood flow ~1.3 ± 0.6 ml/100g/min.; Andersson et al. (2002). International Journal of Obesity. Resting adipose blood flow ~1.0 ± 0.3 ml/100g/min.; See docs/bowhead_references.md for full citation list.

notes: Right whale blubber k=0.20 W/m-K. Right whales have exceptionally thick blubber (15-25 cm) similar to bowhead whales. Bowhead measured at 0.23 W/m-K (North Slope Borough). Slightly lower density due to higher lipid content. Resting subcutaneous adipose perfusion ~1.0-1.3 ml/100g/min (human studies; see docs/right_whale_references.md) converted to ~0.18-0.23 kg/(m3·s); use 0.23 as proxy for blubber baseline. Species perfusion_scale models countercurrent heat exchange.
source_reference: North Slope Borough. Bowhead whale energetics and temperature regulation. Blubber conductivity 0.23 W/m-K.; Parry DA (1949). The structure of whale blubber. Q J Microsc Sci 90:13-25.; Right whales have blubber up to 38 cm thick (Ohmura et al. 1969).; Muller et al. (2014). Circulation. Resting subcutaneous adipose blood flow ~1.3 ± 0.6 ml/100g/min.; Andersson et al. (2002). International Journal of Obesity. Resting adipose blood flow ~1.0 ± 0.3 ml/100g/min.; See docs/right_whale_references.md for full citation list.
